desc: A friendly, small and distictive dragonfly who is easily photographed.

fm.Male: Eyes green-blue, face white, thorax yellow and black striped abdomen pruinescent.
fm.Female: Eyes reddish, face white, thorax like male, abdomen yellow and black striped longitudinally.
fm.Similar Species: Females and younger males fairly unique, pruinescent males could superficially resmble others.

desc: Males are conspicuous and easily identified by black wing patches on mid-wing. Females have 12 spots on wing but differ from 12-spotted Skimmer in those marks and abdomen pattern.

fm.Male: Pale Blue/white abdomen, dark head and thorax.  Wings with large dark panels and some white at base of HW.
fm.Female: eyes, face and thorax dark. Two white thoracic stripes, white/yellow spots down side of abdomen, three patches per wing.
fm.Similar Species: Male Widow Skimmer has black interior on wings. Female 12-spotted Skimmer with bolder abdomen markings.

desc: One of our most distinctive female Dragonflies. Females are unmistakable, but males can resemble other blue and green species.  Look always for pale cersus at tip of abdomen.

fm.Male: Eyes and face green, thorax and abdomen pruinescent, legs white.
fm.Female: Face, eyes and thorax green, abdomen segments based green with black distally.
fm.Similar Species: Females unique in our area. Male Blue Dashers have white face and different perching behavior.
fm.Habitat: Any vegetated water, fond of heavily vegetated ponds.

desc: Face color is key for Meadowhawks ID. Males are entirely red, females are very colorful and lack red.  Females pose a real ID challenge and amount of black on abdomen is key to separating from other red Meadowhawks.

fm.Male: All red with narrow black median on S9
fm.Female: Tan or Tan-reddish otherwise similarly patterned.
fm.Similar Species: White-face, Ruby and Cherry-faced have prominent black in abdomens
fm.Habitat: Ponds and Lakes with lots of vegetation or nearby forests.

desc: An easily identified dragonfly owing to it's wing pattern.

fm.Male: Large black patches basally on both FW & HW. Eyes, face and thorax dark, abdomen pruinescent.
fm.Female: Wings like male but paler. has wishbone pattern yellow stripe down thorax branching on abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Black Saddlebags has dark limited to HW, Common Whitetail with black midway on wings.

desc: A delightful tiny, colorful winged dragonfly who is common at marshy, vernal ponds.

fm.Male: Orange winged and tiny should separate it from anything else.
fm.Female: Orange edge wings have sever brownish splotches. Brownish-red small body.
fm.Similar Species: none.

desc: A large skimmer with distinctive wing pattern.  But beware look-a-likes like the female Common Whitetail.  The shape of spots in wing will make this ID easy.

fm.Male: Has three dark spots per wing interspersed with pruinescent patches.
fm.Female: Has three dark spots per wing and long yellow lines down abdomen
fm.Similar Species: Female Common Whitetail has yellowish spots on side of abdomen and is smaller.

desc: An essential part of Summer in MontCo. This is our most frequently encountered darner but is not as numerous as other species in habitat.  It is a very big species, so no real chance of confusion with similarly colored Eastern Pondhawks or Blue Dashers who perch more and are smaller.

fm.Male: Eyes and thora green, abdomen blue with dorsal black widening towards tip.
fm.Female: Thorax and S1-3 typically green, rest of abdomen mostly brownish.
fm.Similar Species: Great Pondhawk also green but abdomen ringed with green

desc: Females pose the real identification challenge here

fm.Male: Blue eyes, white face and at maturity entirely pruinescent body.
fm.Female: Brownish eyes, pale thorax and two yellow stripes down sides of abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Female's white face and pale legs key for separating from Slaty and Bar-winged Skimmers.

desc: Males are darkly pruinescent, with dark eyes and face and use conspicuous perches to defend territory.

fm.Male: Eyes brown, face dark blue, body blue-blackish.
fm.Female: Eyes brown, face tan or pale, thorax pale sided abdomen with two yellow stripes.
fm.Similar Species: Great Blue Females have white face and paler legs, Bar-winged has dark face.
fm.Habitat: Wooded lakes and streams, sometimes swampy areas.

desc: A standout, brightly colored Skimmer

fm.Male: Wings orangish with two to three brown splotches. Eyes brown, face orange, thorax brown with two pales stripes, abdomen mostly yellow.
fm.Female: Wings yellowish with 2-3 brown splotches. Brownish body, reddish eyes.
fm.Similar Species: Should stand out as unique in our area.
fm.Habitat: Boggy ponds, ditches with vegetation, often near woods.

desc: A standout and fairly unique very large species in our area.

fm.Male: Eyes blue, face brown, thorax brown with green stripes, abdomen brown with 2-3 narrow green rings per segment.
fm.Female: Similar to male, eyes much less blue.
fm.Similar Species: unique in MontCo.
fm.Habitat: Swamps or slow moving streams with adjacent woodland.

desc: A colorfully patterned winged dragonfly, easy to identify and very approachable.

fm.Male: Wings golden with three brownish stripes, wingtip pale. Eyes reddish, face orange, body orange and black.
fm.Female: Wings like male. Eyes red, face pale orange, Thorax yellow, abdomen black with yellow stripe.
fm.Similar Species: Hard to confuse this one, Painted Skimmer yellower with dark tipped wings, Amberwing tiny.

desc: Our most common Mosaic Darner.

fm.Male: Eyes turquoise, body dark. thoracic stripes narrow, blue above greenish-yellow, has narrow frontal stripe, abdomen spots small.
fm.Female: Polymorphic. Likely to see a brown individual with yellowish-green markings, eyes brown.
fm.Similar Species: Black-tipped also in our area is longer with bluer markings.
fm.Habitat: Wide range of slow moving and still water.

desc: The only Saddlebag that is black, easy to identify on the wing.  It rarely perches so get a picture if you find one stopped!

fm.Male: Black interior patch on HW. Dark head and body, abdomen black except S7 which is yellow.
fm.Female: Browner, more yellow on abdomen.  Otherwise like male.
fm.Similar Species: Not easily confused with other dragonflies.
fm.Habitat: Shallow ponds, wanders far from water.

desc: A reddish-brown big bodied Glider

fm.Male: Eyes gray and red, thorax brown/red with two gray stripes, abdomen brownish. Hindwings with small splotch on base.
fm.Female: Similar to male, less red in face.
fm.Similar Species: Wandering Glider is more brightly yellow.
fm.Habitat: Likes to Wander and can be seen anywhere.

desc: The only red Saddlebag likely to be encountered in MontCo.

fm.Male: HW with large red interior patch. Eye and face redish purple, thorax red-brown, abdomen bright red.
fm.Female: Female a less brightly colored version of male with less red in HW.
fm.Similar Species: Red Saddlebags unlikely in our area - has solid red face.
fm.Habitat: Wide variety of fish free water sources.

desc: Both sexes have a horn-like structure at their occiput, reminiscent of a Unicorn. Cersus pale or white.

fm.Male: Eyes blue-green, face pale, thorax striped on front otherwise mostly pale green, abdomen with yellow points, S8-9 black, S10 pale.
fm.Female: Similar to male, more patterned on abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Other clubtails in our area should be darker
fm.Habitat: Beaver ponds, lakes with muddy bottoms and little vegetation.

desc: A yellow dragonfly that hardly ever stops flying

fm.Male: Reddish eyes, orange face, yellow body that is orange dorsally, some black marks on abdomen.
fm.Female: A more yellow version of the male.
fm.Similar Species: Spot-winged Glider darker
fm.Habitat: Wanders widely. Appreciates any water source.

desc: A very small clubtail is possibly it's most distinctive feature.

fm.Male: Eyes green/blue, face yellow, throax with four black stripes, abdomen dark with yellow rings, cerci white.
fm.Female: Similar to male, more patterned on abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: No other Clubtail in our area this small
fm.Habitat: Small clear creeks with sandy shore line and sunny rocks for perching.

desc: Long Clubtail with dark black front of the thorax.

fm.Male: Eyes green, face yellow, thorax mostly yellow-gray but boldly black on front, abdomen moderately clubbed with yellow-gray lines down middle.
fm.Female: Female similar to male, thicker abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Long legs and distinctive thoracic pattern should be distinctive for our area.

desc: A dark long bodied Clubtail.

fm.Male: Eyes green, face pale to brown, thorax with alternating thick and narrow pale stripes, abdomen mostly dark with some pale dorsal marks and on side of club.
fm.Female: Similar to male, abdomen more patterned and lacking club.
fm.Similar Species: In our area this dark, long bodied, narrow clubbed dragonfly should be distinctive.
fm.Habitat: Rivers with current, especially through wooded areas.

desc: Dark wing bands distinctive among red Meadowhawks

fm.Male: Wings with deep red-brown bands on HW. Eyes brownish-red as is thorax, abdomen bright red.
fm.Female: Similar to male but reddish above and yellow-brown or brown below.
fm.Similar Species: Size and wing coloration is unique

desc: Small reddish or yellow Pennant with spotted wings.

fm.Male: Wings with large red interior splotch on HW, smaller spots distally and on FW. Eyes and face red, body red and black.
fm.Female: Wings similar to male with orange stigma. Eyes red, face yellow, body yellow with black spots.
fm.Similar Species: Interior HW patch and size should separate it from anything else.

desc: Pennsylvania listed as Imperiled or Vulnerable.

fm.Male: Slender dark with blue spotted abdomen, thorax with narrow bluish-green stripes, eyes blue.
fm.Female: Similar to male with very long apparent cerci.
fm.Similar Species: Shadow Darner in our area is more likely, but smaller.

desc: Very similar to Mosaic Darners, but with dull smaller eyes.

fm.Male: Eyes brown with little blue, thorax dark with pale yellow or white stripes, abdomen with blue spots.
fm.Female: A duller version of male appearance, sometimes with green abdomenal spots.
fm.Similar Species: Shadow and Black-tipped with bluer eyes and brighter thoracic stripes.

desc: A dark spotted darner.

fm.Male: Dark eyes and abdomen, wing base with small dark splotch, thorax with two pale spots.
fm.Female: female similar, thicker abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Unlikely to be confused.

desc: A massive Clubtail with bold yellow and black markings, an Odonate predator too!

fm.Male: Small green eyes, yellow face, thorax boldly black & yellow striped, abdomen with yellow on side and triangles dorsally.
fm.Female: Similar to male, abdomen thicker and yellower.
fm.Similar Species: Most other clubtails are smaller

desc: A crepuscular species that perches during the day.

fm.Male: Brown eyes and body, white median line. Wings with dark splotch at base.
fm.Female: Similar to male, more patterned on abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Only Shadowdragon in our area, Baskettails with more robust abdomen.

desc: A dark narrow bodied Emerald, Imperiled in Pennsylvania.

fm.Male: Green eyes, metallic dark thorax and dark abdomen with yellow side spots.
fm.Female: Like male but not as metallic.
fm.Similar Species: Range separates most look-a-like Emeralds.

desc: Our most patriotic Meadowhawk, males should be distinctive.  Females ID'd with care looking at amount of black on abdomen segments.

fm.Male: Face and eyes blue, thorax white-gray and abdomen orange-red with black.
fm.Female: Females tan faced and eyes with limited blue, abdomen tan with black marks at end of segments 3-9
fm.Similar Species: Light tan legs and white-gray thorax should separate from other Meadowhawks.
fm.Habitat: Wide range of slow moving and still water often near wood land.

desc: Possibly our most common Clubtail.

fm.Male: Blue eyes, thorax more dark than yellow, abdomen with dorsal triangles and yellow on sides.
fm.Female: Similar to male but paler in yellow color.
fm.Similar Species: Very similar to Ashy

desc: The lone red "Green Darner" of the group. This one is a strong flyer like Green Darner but even a blurry in-flight photo can be diagnostic.

fm.Male: Green face, eyes and thorax, abdomen orange-red.
fm.Female: Similar to male but abdomen speckled with green spots.
fm.Similar Species: Unlikely to be confused.
fm.Habitat: Shallow lakes and ponds lacking fish, with plenty of vegetation.

desc: A long narrowbodied Baskettail with bold wing markings

fm.Male: Eyes green, thorax and abdomen mostly dark, wings typically with four spots each.
fm.Female: female similar, thicker abdomen. Eyes gray-brown
fm.Similar Species: Common Whitetail and 12-spotted Skimmer smaller with different body shape.
fm.Habitat: Larger creeks and rivers or ponds with sandy banks.

desc: One of three possible small red dragonflies in our area.  It's face is mostly white - not tan or red.

fm.Male: Face white, eyes deep burgundy, thorax red, abdomen red with black triangles on side.
fm.Female: brownish-yellow. Face white, eyes brownish-red on top, black abdomenal markings like male
fm.Similar Species: Ruby and Cherry-faced likely in our area too
fm.Habitat: Wet marshes and bogs even when dry

desc: A dark, brownish Clubtail with minimal club

fm.Male: Blue eyes, dark thorax with a 2-3 pale stripes, abdomen with narrow triangular pattern dorsally.
fm.Female: Similar to male
fm.Similar Species: Lancet is smaller, Dusky not in our area
fm.Habitat: Slow-flowing wooded streams and rivers, edge of big lakes

desc: A large black and yellow dragonfly with dramatic green eyes.

fm.Male: Yellow abdomen rings, face yellow & black, thorax with two front stripes and two on side.
fm.Female: female similar, thicker abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Other spiketails lack abdomen rings, clubtails have clubs.

desc: This Brown Cruiser flies earlier than larger River Cruisers.

fm.Male: Single pale thorax stripe, eyes brownish green, abdomen forming small club.
fm.Female: Female similar, abdomen lacks club and has broader paler basal segment spots.
fm.Similar Species: Size and single stripe distinctive, mottled female could be confused with Darner.

desc: A large blue-eyed darner with broad green thoracic stripes on side and front of thorax. Abdomen dark with blue dorsally and spotting on side.

fm.Male: Blue eyes, dark thorax with two broad green stripes in side. Front of thorax has "L-shaped" green stripe. Abdomen dark with blue dorsally and spotting on side.
fm.Female: Similar to male
fm.Similar Species: Resembles Mosaic Darners but comhination of eye color and broad thoracic stripes should separate them.
